text.title: Laravel PHPUnit / Pest Testing Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the layered PHPUnit / Pest testing methodology for Laravel. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Feature tests MUST live under `tests/Feature/` and use `TestCase` (boots Laravel). Unit tests MUST live under `tests/Unit/` and extend `PHPUnit\Framework\TestCase` (no framework boot).
rule.rationale: The directory + base class encodes intent; mixing slows the suite + makes intent invisible.

rule.statement: Feature tests touching the database MUST use `RefreshDatabase` (or `DatabaseMigrations` for full reset). The test DB connection in `phpunit.xml` MUST be a real engine (Postgres / MySQL via Testcontainers or CI service), not sqlite, when production differs.
rule.rationale: Sqlite tests pass while Postgres prod fails on JSON ops + case sensitivity; `RefreshDatabase` keeps tests isolated.

rule.statement: `Mail::fake()`, `Queue::fake()`, `Event::fake()`, `Http::fake()`, `Bus::fake()` MUST be called BEFORE the action under test. Fakes called after the SUT runs are a no-op for the just-fired effect.
rule.rationale: Faking after dispatch lets the real effect (mail, HTTP call) fire; the assertion then fails or — worse — passes when the real side effect did happen.

rule.statement: JSON response assertions MUST use `assertJsonPath('data.id', 1)` for value checks. `assertJsonStructure` is allowed only when verifying key existence in deeply nested responses; never as a substitute for value checks.
rule.rationale: `assertJsonStructure` passes as long as keys exist; it accepts wrong values and prints a misleading green.

rule.statement: Each test method MUST exercise exactly one behaviour. The method (or `it(...)` / `test(...)` description) MUST state the behaviour in present-tense English (Pest) or `methodUnderTest_state_expectedBehaviour` (PHPUnit).

rationale.source: Roy Osherove — Art of Unit Testing

rule.rationale: Multi-behaviour tests hide regressions; one-per-test makes CI output a bug report.

rule.statement: CI MUST enforce coverage threshold: `pest --coverage --min=80` (or equivalent PHPUnit). Lowering the threshold as a "quick fix" is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Coverage without a gate is advisory; agents skip uncovered paths unless the build blocks.
